description: Canonical authentication native-error -> public JS AuthErrorCode mapping. The iOS classifier (SpotifyAuthErrorMapping.classify) and Android authenticateAsync must stay aligned with this matrix. Rules are listed in classifier priority order: lower `priority` is evaluated first, so typed/structural signals win over best-effort text heuristics.
passthroughCodesDescription: Public codes thrown directly by a dedicated code path rather than produced by the ambiguous-error classifier `rules` below, so they intentionally have no classifier rule. REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRED is raised by SpotifyTokenRefreshClient (iOS) / SpotifyTokenSwapClient (Android) on an `invalid_grant` refresh response; the others are raised at their throw sites (AuthInProgress/InvalidConfig/SpotifyNotInstalled checks).

rules.when: User dismissed the auth UI. Typed cancellation domain/code anywhere in the NSError chain, or (best-effort) cancel text within a known auth/browser domain.
Transport failure anywhere in the chain (NSURLErrorDomain, non-cancellation code).
OAuth-style rejection text, or parsed HTTP 401/403. Evaluated before the token-swap gate so OAuth rejections are not misclassified as token-swap failures.
Token-swap context (swap URL configured or token-endpoint text) AND parse/decode failure text.
Token-swap context with an HTTP status, or any other token-swap-class failure.
Nothing else matched. The original NSError is preserved as `cause`.
